channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to Shiri Bibas and her 2 young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us,” tens of thousands of Israelis lined highways as the bodies of a mother and her two young sons, killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip, were taken for burial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greene threatens criminal referrals at House DOGE hearing
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.), chair of the House Oversight Subcommittee on the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), threatened to make criminal referrals during a hearing on foreign aid on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: High-level EU-US diplomatic talks are called off as transatlantic tensions rise
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned meeting between European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Secretary of State Marco Rubio was abruptly canceled Wednesday due to “scheduling issues,” coming as political tensions have increased between Europe and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bosnian Serb leader Dodik is convicted and sentenced for separatist activities as tensions surge
item.description: SARAJEVO, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — A court in Bosnia on Wednesday sentenced the pro-Russia Bosnian Serb president, Milorad Dodik, to one year in prison and banned him from politics for six years over his separatist actions as tensions mount in the fragile Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: AMER COHADZIC, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Romanian prosecutors launch investigation into far-right populist who led canceled presidential vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian prosecutors launched a criminal investigation on Wednesday against a far-right populist who won the first round in a canceled presidential election last year, accusing him of election campaign funding abuses, supporting fascist groups and other offenses.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN food agency pauses aid to famine-hit Sudan displacement camp of half a million people
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations’ food agency says it has paused aid distribution in Sudan’s famine-hit Zamzam displacement camp of a half-million people as fighting intensifies between the country’s warring sides, and it warns that thousands could now starve.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Romanian far-right presidential frontrunner taken into custody after JD Vance's rebuke of European elections
item.description: Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu is being questioned by prosecutors after Vice President JD Vance criticized a "Soviet-era" shift in European democracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Keir Starmer heads to Washington with UK defense spending pledge to help sway Trump over Ukraine
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is flying to Washington on a trip aimed at persuading U.S. President Donald Trump to maintain military support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death toll in Sudan military plane crash rises to 46
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The death toll from a Sudanese military aircraft crash in the city of Omdurman increased to at least 46 people, including women and children, officials said Wednesday, one of the deadliest plane crashes in the northeastern African nation in the past two decades.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff may travel to Middle East Sunday for Israel-Hamas ceasefire talks
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s special envoy for the Middle East, may travel to the region on Sunday if indirect talks between Israel and Hamas on a second phase of a ceasefire deal show progress this week.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan dispatches navy, air force after China launches live-fire drills with no warning
item.description: Taiwan's defense ministry says China launched live-fire drills barely 40 miles off the coast of Taiwan on Wednesday with no prior warning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Analysts back Trump’s USAID cuts in Africa, say increased trade will better benefit continent's poor
item.description: Some analysts believe that President Donald Trump was right to cut aid to Africa and that increased trade with the continent will fill "the plates of hungry kids at the end of the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis shows further slight improvement as he battles pneumonia, the Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis has shown further slight improvement as he battles double pneumonia, but doctors still say his prognosis is still guarded.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield And Silvia Stellacci, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on new exchange, leaving fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials said Tuesday they have reached an agreement to exchange the bodies of dead hostages for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, keeping their fragile ceasefire intact for at least a few more days.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trapped in Gaza: Residents speak out as Egypt blocks exit, rejects Trump’s relocation plan
item.description: Gazans trapped by closed borders voice frustration, with some supporting Trump’s relocation plan as Egypt blocks exits and Hamas clings to control amid devastation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis shows 'slight improvement' in kidney failure battle
item.description: Pope Francis' 10th night at Gemelli Hospital in Rome was quiet and restful as he battles early signs of kidney failure, the Vatican said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis remains in 'critical condition' with signs of early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis remains hospitalized in "critical condition" with pneumonia, but the Vatican says he rested well overnight.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9
